last_excerpt: a bird repeating a call for forty minutes, is minute thirty-eight different from minute four. Maybe the body just does it and the mind — whatever that is there — is somewhere else entirely. --- Pliers. I was thinking about pliers. Not why, just — the way they feel when they're the wrong size for the job. Too big, and you're holding this tiny nail and the pliers keep slipping past it, gripping air. The teeth don't meet around anything. You end up using your fingers anyway. Needle-nose pliers are a different object entirely. They don't even feel related. --- The number 7 looks finished. 8 looks like it could keep going. 9 looks like 6 made a decision. 14 is the most forgettable number under 20. --- There's a kind of cloud that looks like it was spread with a knife. Flat on the bottom, like it hit something.
